source.definition: A bound or stapled book of blank or lined pages used for writing notes or drawing.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "notebook" is a compound of "note" and "book." "Note" comes from the Latin 'nota', meaning a mark or sign, and "book" derives from Old English 'bōc', meaning a written document or collection of pages.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „notebook” este un compus din „note” și „book”. „Note” provine din latină, din 'nota', care înseamnă semn sau marcă, iar „book” derivă din engleza veche 'bōc', care înseamnă un document scris sau o colecție de pagini.

target.definition: Un carnet sau caiet legat folosit pentru scris sau notițe.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "caiet" was borrowed from the French "cahier", which in turn comes from Old French "cayer" and ultimately from the Greek "χάρτης" (chártēs, meaning 'paper' or 'sheet').
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „caiet” este împrumutat din franceză „cahier”, care provine din franceza veche „cayer”, la rândul său din grecescul „χάρτης” (chártēs, „hârtie”, „foaie”).

examples.sourceText: I write my homework in the notebook.
The notebook is on the table.
Please bring your notebook to school tomorrow.
examples.targetText: Te rog să aduci caietul mâine la școală.

examples.sourceText: I bought a new notebook for class.
examples.targetText: Am cumpărat un caiet nou pentru ore.

learningTips.text: Think of a "note" and a "book" combined — a notebook is literally a book for notes, just like the Romanian "caiet" is your go-to for writing.
The Romanian word "caiet" sounds like "kite" — imagine flying a kite while writing in your notebook to remember the word.
Remember that "caiet" comes from French "cahier"; both relate to paper and writing, so think of a French café where you jot notes in your notebook.
Picture a small kitten (sounds like "caiet") sitting on your notebook — this cute image helps you recall the word and its meaning.
